page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-16 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 128 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Cache resources include 32 KB of L1 data cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25% at 2927.28. Passmark memory testing also registers in the top 25% with a score of 2956.3. Multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics generally fall within the average range, including a multi-core stress-ng score of 23475.85 and uncached large-block read bandwidth of 85.94 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the server achieves a strong text generation speed of 267.48 tokens per second using a small 135M LLM model, though performance drops to a poor 3.61 tokens per second for prompt processing on a large 70B model.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c2d-highmem-16 offers a high-density memory profile relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The combination of dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 128 GB of RAM is suited for database operations, software compilation, web serving, and moderate-scale language model inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-16 is an x86_64 server instance configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 8 physical cores, it features 32 MB of L3 cache but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance and memory scores, both placing in the top 25%, while multi-core execution and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. The instance demonstrates strong text generation speeds for small language models but struggles with larger 70B models. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and software compilation.
